s: Profound hearing loss due to meningitis

d: has a chronic disease that requires medication every day and causes some worry but minimal interference with daily activities.

h: Generic uncomplicated disease: worry and daily medication

s: Mild heart failure due to rheumatic heart disease
h: Heart failure, mild
d: is short of breath and easily tires with moderate physical activity, such as walking uphill or more than a quarter-mile on level ground. The person feels comfortable at rest or during activities requiring less effort.

s: Moderate heart failure due to rheumatic heart disease
h: Heart failure, moderate
d: is short of breath and easily tires with minimal physical activity, such as walking only a short distance. The person feels comfortable at rest but avoids moderate activity.

s: Severe heart failure due to rheumatic heart disease
h: Heart failure, severe
d: is short of breath and feels tired when at rest. The person avoids any physical activity, for fear of worsening the breathing problems.

s: Controlled, medically managed heart failure due to rheumatic heart disease
h: Generic uncomplicated disease: worry and daily medication
d: has a chronic disease that requires medication every day and causes some worry but minimal interference with daily activities.

h: Asymptomatic

s: Mild angina due to ischemic heart disease
h: Angina pectoris, mild
d: has chest pain that occurs with strenuous physical activity, such as running or lifting heavy objects. After a brief rest, the pain goes away.

s: Moderate angina due to ischemic heart disease
h: Angina pectoris, moderate
d: has chest pain that occurs with moderate physical activity, such as walking uphill or more than half a kilometer (around a quarter-mile) on level ground. After a brief rest, the pain goes away.

s: Severe angina due to ischemic heart disease
h: Angina pectoris, severe
d: has chest pain that occurs with minimal physical activity, such as walking only a short distance. After a brief rest, the pain goes away. The person avoids most physical activities because of the pain.

s: Asymptomatic angina due to ischemic heart disease
h: Asymptomatic

s: Acute myocardial infarction first 2 days
h: Acute myocardial infarction, days 1-2
d: has severe chest pain that becomes worse with any physical activity,. The person feels nauseous, short of breath, and very anxious.

s: Acute myocardial infarction 3 to 28 days
h: Acute myocardial infarction, days 3-28
d: gets short of breath after heavy physical activity, and tires easily, but has no problems when at rest. The person has to take medication every day and has some anxiety.

d: has severe vision loss, which causes difficulty in daily activities, some emotional impact (for example worry), and some difficulty going outside the home without assistance.

s: Blindness due to macular degeneration
h: Distance vision blindness
d: is completely blind, which causes great difficulty in some daily activities, worry and anxiety, and great difficulty going outside the home without assistance.

s: Moderate vision impairment due to uncorrected refractive error
h: Distance vision, moderate impairment
d: has vision problems that make it difficult to recognize faces or objects across a room.

s: Severe vision impairment due to uncorrected refractive error
h: Distance vision, severe impairment
d: has severe vision loss, which causes difficulty in daily activities, some emotional impact (for example worry), and some difficulty going outside the home without assistance.

s: Blindness due to uncorrected refractive error
h: Distance vision blindness
d: is completely blind, which causes great difficulty in some daily activities, worry and anxiety, and great difficulty going outside the home without assistance.

s: Near vision loss
h: Presbyopia
d: has difficulty seeing things that are nearer than 3 feet, but has no difficulty with seeing things at a distance.

s: Moderate vision impairment due to other vision loss
h: Distance vision, moderate impairment
d: has vision problems that make it difficult to recognize faces or objects across a room.

s: Severe vision impairment due to other vision loss
h: Distance vision, severe impairment
d: has severe vision loss, which causes difficulty in daily activities, some emotional impact (for example worry), and some difficulty going outside the home without assistance.

s: Blindness due to other vision loss
h: Distance vision blindness

s: Mild hearing loss due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, mild
d: has great difficulty hearing and understanding another person talking in a noisy place (for example, on an urban street).

s: Moderate hearing loss due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, moderate
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking in a noisy place (for example, on an urban street), and has difficulty hearing another person talking even in a quiet place or on the phone.

s: Severe hearing loss due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, severe
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking, even in a quiet place, and unable to take part in a phone conversation. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others cause emotional impact at times (for example worry or depression).

s: Profound hearing loss due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, profound
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking, even in a quiet place, is unable to take part in a phone conversation, and has great difficulty hearing anything in any other situation. Difficulties with communicating and relating to othersoften cause worry, depression, and loneliness.

s: Complete hearing loss due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, complete
d: cannot hear at all in any situation, including even the loudest sounds, and cannot communicate verbally or use a phone. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others often cause worry, depression or loneliness.

s: Mild hearing loss with ringing due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, mild, with ringing
d: has great difficulty hearing and understanding another person talking in a noisy place (for example, on an urban street), and sometimes has annoying ringing in the ears.

s: Moderate hearing loss with ringing due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, moderate, with ringing
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking in a noisy place (for example, on an urban street), and has difficulty hearing another person talking even in a quiet place or on the phone, and has annoying ringing in the ears for more than 5 minutes at a time, almost everyday.

s: Severe hearing loss with ringing due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, severe, with ringing
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking, even in a quiet place, and unable to take part in a phone conversation, and has annoying ringing in the ears for more than 5 minutes at a time, almost everyday. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others cause emotional impact at times (for example worry or depression).

s: Profound hearing loss with ringing due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, profound, with ringing
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking, even in a quiet place, is unable to take part in a phone conversation, has great difficulty hearing anything in any other situation, and has annoying ringing in the ears for more than 5 minutes at a time, several times a day. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others often cause worry, depression, or loneliness.

s: Complete hearing loss with ringing due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, complete, with ringing
d: cannot hear at all in any situation, including even the loudest sounds, and cannot communicate verbally or use a phone, and has very annoying ringing in the ears for more than half of the day. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others often cause worry, depression or loneliness.

s: Moderately severe hearing loss due to age-related and other hearing loss
h: Hearing loss, moderately severe
d: (custom DW from hearing loss impairment envelope)

h: Hearing loss, moderately severe, with ringing

s: Mild intellectual disability due to encephalocele
h: Intellectual disability / mental retardation, mild

s: Mild hearing loss due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, mild
d: has great difficulty hearing and understanding another person talking in a noisy place (for example, on an urban street).

s: Moderate hearing loss due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, moderate
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking in a noisy place (for example, on an urban street), and has difficulty hearing another person talking even in a quiet place or on the phone.

s: Severe hearing loss due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, severe
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking, even in a quiet place, and unable to take part in a phone conversation. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others cause emotional impact at times (for example worry or depression).

s: Profound hearing loss due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, profound
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking, even in a quiet place, is unable to take part in a phone conversation, and has great difficulty hearing anything in any other situation. Difficulties with communicating and relating to othersoften cause worry, depression, and loneliness.

s: Complete hearing loss due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, complete
d: cannot hear at all in any situation, including even the loudest sounds, and cannot communicate verbally or use a phone. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others often cause worry, depression or loneliness.

s: Mild hearing loss with ringing due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, mild, with ringing
d: has great difficulty hearing and understanding another person talking in a noisy place (for example, on an urban street), and sometimes has annoying ringing in the ears.

s: Moderate hearing loss with ringing due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, moderate, with ringing
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking in a noisy place (for example, on an urban street), and has difficulty hearing another person talking even in a quiet place or on the phone, and has annoying ringing in the ears for more than 5 minutes at a time, almost everyday.

s: Severe hearing loss with ringing due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, severe, with ringing
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking, even in a quiet place, and unable to take part in a phone conversation, and has annoying ringing in the ears for more than 5 minutes at a time, almost everyday. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others cause emotional impact at times (for example worry or depression).

s: Profound hearing loss with ringing due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, profound, with ringing
d: is unable to hear and understand another person talking, even in a quiet place, is unable to take part in a phone conversation, has great difficulty hearing anything in any other situation, and has annoying ringing in the ears for more than 5 minutes at a time, several times a day. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others often cause worry, depression, or loneliness.

s: Complete hearing loss with ringing due to other congenital anomalies
h: Hearing loss, complete, with ringing
d: cannot hear at all in any situation, including even the loudest sounds, and cannot communicate verbally or use a phone, and has very annoying ringing in the ears for more than half of the day. Difficulties with communicating and relating to others often cause worry, depression or loneliness.

s: Post-acute fatigue syndrome due to COVID-19
h: Infectious disease, post-acute consequences (fatigue, emotional lability, insomnia)
d: is always tired and easily upset. The person feels pain all over the body and is depressed.

s: Post-acute mild cognitive symptoms due to COVID-19
h: Dementia, mild
d: has some trouble remembering recent events, and finds it hard to concentrate and make decisions and plans.

s: Post-acute severe cognitive symptoms due to COVID-19
h: Dementia, moderate
d: has memory problems and confusion, feels disoriented, at times hears voices that are not real, and needs help with some daily activities.

s: Post-acute mild respiratory symptoms due to COVID-19
h: COPD and other chronic respiratory problems, mild
d: has cough and shortness of breath after heavy physical activity, but is able to walk long distances and climb stairs.

s: Post-acute moderate respiratory symptoms due to COVID-19
h: COPD and other chronic respiratory problems, moderate
d: has cough, wheezing and shortness of breath, even after light physical activity. The person feels tired and can walk only short distances or climb only a few stairs.

s: Post-acute severe respiratory symptoms due to COVID-19
h: COPD and other chronic respiratory problems, severe
d: has cough, wheezing and shortness of breath all the time. The person has great difficulty walking even short distances or climbing any stairs, feels tired when at rest, and is anxious.

s: Asymptomatic COVID-19 cases
h: Asymptomatic

s: Mild acute COVID-19
h: Infectious disease, acute episode, mild
d: has a low fever and mild discomfort , but no difficulty with daily activities.

s: Moderate acute COVID-19
h: Infectious disease, acute episode, moderate
d: has a fever and aches, and feels weak, which causes some difficulty with daily activities.

s: Severe acute COVID-19
h: Infectious disease, acute episode, severe
d: has a high fever and pain, and feels very weak, which causes great difficulty with daily activities.

s: Critical acute COVID-19
h: Intensive care unit admission
d: is very ill and often asleep or unconscious; when awake cannot move in bed, cannot speak, is completely dependent on others and is anxious

s: Guillain-Barré Syndrome due to COVID-19
h: Spinal cord lesion below neck level (treated)
d: is paralyzed from the waist down, cannot feel or move the legs and has difficulties with urine and bowel control. The person uses a wheelchair to move around.
